items.title: Visually impaired teen scores 8As in SPM
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A visually impaired teenager from Keningau scored 8As in the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination despite struggling with blurred vision since childhood. Eighteen-year-old Alexzander Dinus said a nerve problem caused him to see multiple images, making it difficult to follow lessons in class. "Sometimes I see three images.

items.title: RBC lifts S&P 500 year-end target to 7,900 on AI optimism
items.summary: NEW YORK: RBC Capital Markets raised its year-end target for the S&P 500 to 7,900 from 7,750 on Friday, citing resilient earnings growth and continued strength in artificial intelligence-linked sectors. RBC said positive earnings revisions, driven by technology and AI-linked firms, alongside strong demand for AI infrastructure have supported valuations. Separately, among sectors, RBC downgraded US healthcare stocks to "market weight" from "overweight" due to earnings revisions, fund outflows and policy uncertainty despite still-attractive valuations.

items.title: Palm flat as traders wait for key data; on track for second weekly loss
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian palm oil futures were largely flat on Friday, as traders preferred to wait for next week's demand and supply data from the palm oil board. The market is largely in consolidation mode, awaiting the release of the Malaysian Palm Oil Board (MPOB) data, said Paramalingam Supramaniam, director at brokerage Pelindung Bestari. Dalian's most-active soyoil contract fell 1.14 per cent, while its palm oil contract shed 0.87 per cent.

items.title: Indonesian authorities rushing to rescue 20 hikers after Mount Dukono eruption
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesian authorities are rushing to rescue 20 hikers after the eruption of Mount Dukono on Halmahera island on Friday, a local rescue agency official told Reuters. Mount Dukono, located in North Maluku province, erupted on Friday at 7.41am local time (1041 GMT Thursday), spewing volcanic ash as high as 10km (6.21 miles) into the sky, the country's volcanology agency said. The agency maintained the alert status at the mountain at its third-highest level, agency head Lana Saria said in a statement.

items.title: Indonesia's FX reserves fall to US$146.2 billion at end-April, lowest in nearly two years
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's forex reserves dropped by US$2 billion in April to US$146.2 billion, the lowest level in nearly two years, due to intervention in the FX market to defend the rupiah amid rising uncertainties, Bank Indonesia said on Friday. BI said reserves were equal to 5.8 months' worth of imports. Indonesia raised US$1.1 billion in a Samurai bond sale in April.

items.title: RTS Link to be nation's busiest gateway; 90pct complete ahead of Dec 31 launch
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor Baru–Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link is set to become Malaysia's busiest land entry point, with operations focused on high-tech security and efficiency ahead of its Dec 31 launch. "Security, immigration control, threat detection, and operational efficiency must be at the highest level from day one. Key security installations include 18 baggage scanners, 22 body scanners, and 10 walkthrough scanners using millimetre wave technology.

items.title: Penang records RM63.96bil in approved investments for 2025
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang strengthened its status as one of Malaysia's main economic growth engines after recording RM63.96 billion in approved investments in 2025, Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib said today. Ramli said the investment composition reflected balanced confidence among both domestic and foreign investors in Penang's economic prospects. Domestic investments totalled RM38.85 billion, while foreign investments stood at RM25.10 billion.

items.title: No compromise on those exploiting 3R issues, says Tengku Mahkota Pahang
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Tengku Mahkota of Pahang, Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah Al-Sultan Abdullah, has issued a stern warning against those attempting to threaten harmony in the state by manipulating sensitive issues. Opening the 15th State Legislative Assembly sitting at Wisma Sri Pahang today, he said there would be no compromise for those who exploit race, religion, and the royal institution (3R). Tengku Hassanal said all quarters, regardless of background, must act responsibly in all matters and uphold the values of respect, tolerance, and the rule of law.

items.title: Penang exco says no misappropriation in RM500,000 fishermen voucher programme, cites document delays
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 8 — Penang state exco Datuk Rashidi Zinol has denied any misappropriation following claims by fishing equipment shop operators that they had not received payments under a RM500,000 fishermen voucher programme. The Rural Development, Agrotechnology, Food Security and Entrepreneurship Committee chairman said there were issues with payments involving various agencies, namely the Malaysian Fisheries Development Authority (LKIM), the State Economic Planning Division (BPEN) and several state financial agencies. He said six of the eight companies had submitted complete documents and would be eligible for payment first.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia in negative territory at midday on uncertain Middle East developments
items.summary: Public Bank Bhd shed three sen to RM4.86, CIMB Group Holdings Bhd gave up five sen to RM7.99, and IHH Healthcare Bhd rose one sen to RM8.89. Among the most active counters, Top Glove Corp Bhd added 1.5 sen to 7.5 sen, ACE Market debutant Inspace Creation Bhd inched up one sen to 26 sen, Hartalega Holdings Bhd gained six sen to RM1.24, and Tanco Holdings Bhd improved two sen to RM1.67. Among the top gainers, Hong Leong Industries Bhd surged RM1.14 to RM19.34, Petronas Dagangan Bhd soared 20 sen to RM20.76, Pentamaster Corp Bhd went up 18 sen to RM4.56, and TIME dotCom Bhd gained 16 sen to RM6.30.

items.title: Bartender charged with drunk driving in fatal crash involving young mother
items.summary: S. Darshan pleaded not guilty after the charge was read before magistrate Uthman Abd Ghani. He was accused of driving with a blood alcohol level above the legal limit, resulting in a collision that killed Nur Aleya Natasha Roslan, 21. Deputy public prosecutor Syauqi Mardia Baharudin proposed bail of RM10,000 and requested the court to impound the accused's driving licence.

items.title: No Malaysians aboard hantavirus-linked cruise ship, says Dzulkefly
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad today said no Malaysians were on board the MV Hondius cruise ship linked to a hantavirus outbreak, which had been en route to Spain’s Canary Islands. Dzulkefly said the Ministry of Health had taken note of reports on the outbreak and had contacted Singapore health authorities this morning for updates. He said Singapore health authorities also confirmed that two Singaporean crew members had tested negative for hantavirus, while contact tracing was ongoing.

items.title: Matrix Concepts bags five property awards at StarProperty Awards 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Matrix Concepts Holdings Bhd has clinched five honours at the StarProperty Real Estate Developer Awards 2026, marking another milestone as the company celebrates its 30th anniversary year. The awards recognised the developer's township projects in Negri Sembilan and Johor. Matrix Concepts said that it celebrates a landmark moment this year as the awards mark its 10th consecutive year of recognition at the StarProperty Awards since 2017.

items.title: Immigration raid at Cyberjaya foreigner hub nets 13
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department detained 13 foreigners during an integrated operation at Menara Paragon in Cyberjaya, described by authorities as a hub for foreigners. "The operation focused on food premises, kiosks, sales centres and business premises suspected of employing or harbouring foreigners without valid permits," he said in a statement today. Zakaria said 16 premises were inspected, involving checks on 105 foreigners and 89 Malaysians.

items.title: Fireman charged with sexual assault of 14-year-old student
items.summary: IPOH: A 28-year-old fireman was charged at the Sessions Court today with seven counts of physical sexual assault and possession of a pornographic image of a 14-year-old student. Muhammad Aidilafifi Muhammad pleaded not guilty to all charges before judge Jean Sharmila Jesudason. The charges fall under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, punishable under the same section read together with Section 16(1).

items.title: Manufacturing sales rise 5.3pct to RM173.1bil in March 2026: Statistics Department
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The manufacturing sector posted sales value of RM173.1 billion in March 2026, an increase of 5.3 per cent year-on-year (YoY), said the Statistics Department. On a month-on-month basis, sales value rose by 8.7 per cent, from RM159.2 billion recorded in February 2026. Meanwhile, Mohd Uzir said sales value growth in the export-oriented industries, which account for 71.7 per cent of total sales, expanded by 6.5 per cent in March 2026, led by the increase in the manufacture of computers, electronics and optical products at 15.2 per cent.

items.title: UAE countering Iranian air attack after Trump says ceasefire still in effect
items.summary: WASHINGTON/CAIRO: US ally the United Arab Emirates said its air defences were engaging missile and drone threats from Iran early on Friday in a further test of the shaky, month-long ceasefire between the US and Iran. Iran has often targeted the UAE and other Gulf countries that host US bases since the war began on February 28. "They trifled with us today.

items.title: LBS wins four major accolades, strengthening position as design-led developer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: LBS Bina Group Bhd has clinched four major accolades at a recent property awards event, reinforcing the group's growing reputation as a leading design-led property developer and reinforcing its long-term strategic direction towards upscale, sustainable and community-centric developments. The property developer received the Foreign Touch Award for Centrum Iris, the Excellence in the Business Estate Award for Alam Perdana Industrial Park, and the Excellence in the Starter Home Award for KITA Sejati at the StarProperty Real Estate Developer Awards 2026. Lucas Lim, deputy chief executive officer of LBS Bina Group Bhd, received the 'Top 10 High Performing Companies Award' from Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming.

items.title: Man arrested 'with weapon' near ex-prince Andrew's home
items.summary: LONDON: UK police said Thursday they had arrested a man wielding a weapon who "was behaving in an intimidating manner" close to the home of former prince Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor in eastern England. Norfolk police confirmed they had been called to an incident late Wednesday near the Sandringham Estate "and the man was arrested on suspicion of a public order offence and possession of an offensive weapon." UK media reported that a man wearing a balaclava had allegedly threatened Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor when he was out walking his dogs in the eastern Sandringham area.

items.title: Penang denies misuse of RM500,000 in fishermen voucher programme
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang government has denied allegations of misappropriation involving a RM500,000 fishermen's voucher redemption programme following a police report lodged by eight suppliers over delayed payments. State Rural Development, Agrotechnology, Food Security and Entrepreneurship Committee chairman Datuk Rashidi Zinol said the delay was due to technical and procedural issues involving several agencies, rather than any misuse of funds. Rashidi said RM293,000 of the RM500,000 allocation had already been paid to the companies.

items.title: Penang yet to decide on RM1b Karpal Singh Drive project as developer seeks extension, says state exco
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 8 — The fate of the proposed Karpal Singh Drive reclamation project remains undecided, with the developer seeking more time to revise and resubmit its plans. The developer, PLB Engineering Berhad, will be applying to project owner, Penang Development Corporation (PDC), for an extension of time (EOT), state local government committee chairman Jason H’ng Mooi Lye said in a news conference at the State Legislative Assembly building here today. H’ng said the state is still evaluating the project following repeated setbacks in its environmental approval process.

items.title: Anwar: Kuala Lumpur Declaration key to strengthening Asean resilience
items.summary: CEBU: The Kuala Lumpur Declaration, adopted by Asean last year, must serve as a key foundation in strengthening the region's economic resilience, energy security and supply chain stability amid ongoing global geopolitical uncertainty, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar said Asean should build on the momentum of the declaration, which reaffirms collective commitments to strategic autonomy, economic resilience and sustainable growth. "The declaration recognises that energy security, food resilience, supply chain stability and stronger regional connectivity are now strategic priorities, not merely sectoral concerns," he said.

items.title: 20 people missing after volcano erupts in east Indonesia
items.summary: Jakarta: Twenty people, nine of them from Singapore, were reported missing Friday after Mount Dukono on Indonesia's eastern Halmahera island erupted, spewing a smoke cloud some 10 kilometres (6.2 miles) into the air, a rescue official told AFP. Based on reports, there are about 20 people being searched for," search and rescue official Iwan Ramdani said by telephone. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Van driver claims trial to reckless driving charge over death of Year Two pupil in Batu Pahat
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, May 8 — A van driver pleaded not guilty in the Magistrate’s Court here today to reckless driving resulting in the death of an eight-year-old girl last Tuesday. Nordin Ithnin, 61, entered the plea before Magistrate Arun Noval Dass. According to the charge, the accused allegedly drove a white van recklessly, causing the death of Nur Aini Umairah Mohd Fareez, who was walking along the road in front of Sekolah Kebangsaan Seri Maimon, Parit Sulong, at about 12.30pm on May 5.

items.title: Three South Koreans in court over RM39.6mil Genting Highlands crypto-kidnapping
items.summary: RAUB: Three South Korean men were produced at the Sessions Court today to face charges for the high-profile kidnapping of a compatriot in Genting Highlands last month, involving a cryptocurrency ransom worth RM39.66 million. The kidnappers allegedly demanded a ransom of 10 million Tether (USDT) from the victim's family. The family reportedly paid 3 million USDT, equivalent to approximately RM11.9 million, to secure his release.

items.title: Police: Woman crashes car into Tumpat laundry shop after pressing wrong pedal
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 8 — A woman is believed to have accidentally pressed the accelerator while making a U-turn which caused the car to crash into a laundry shop at Pasar Besar Wakaf Bharu, Tumpat, yesterday. Tumpat district police chief ACP Mohd Khairi Shafie said the 11.20am incident involved a Honda City driven by a local woman in her 50s. He said initial investigations found that the woman was driving from Jalan Puteri Saadong towards the laundry premises before making a U-turn at the scene of the incident.

items.title: No ‘tebuk atap’ in Negeri Sembilan, says Mat Sabu as Amanah backs PKR’s Aminuddin as MB
items.summary: “No way Amanah will lead the ‘tebuk atap’ in Negeri Sembilan. The menteri besar Negeri Sembilan is currently Datuk Aminuddin. “And Amanah’s sole elected representative in Negeri Sembilan must support Datuk Aminuddin.

items.title: Penang must not be a 'mere observer' amid global uncertainty, says TYT
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang cannot afford to remain a mere observer amid growing global economic and geopolitical uncertainties, says Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib. Opening the first meeting of the fourth term of the 15th Penang State Legislative Assembly today, Ramli said the state must take proactive measures to strengthen its resilience and internal capabilities. Ramli said prolonged tensions in several strategic regions, including in the Middle East, had triggered chain reactions affecting global market stability, particularly in the energy sector and supply chains.

items.title: Tengku Hassanal: Federal govt must restore Pahang's rights over sand exports
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Tengku Mahkota of Pahang, Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah Al-Sultan Abdullah, has expressed deep disappointment over the federal government's decision to ban river sand exports, saying the move has hindered state revenue growth. However, the federal government no longer allows it, even though land and sand resources are state rights. Tengku Hassanal said the federal government should not overlook Pahang's contributions as the largest state in Peninsular Malaysia.

items.title: ‘We hope it can help others’: Family redirects donated leukaemia medication after daughter dies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — The leukaemia treatment medication obtained through public donations for a female patient from Limbang, Sarawak, will be donated to another needy recipient after the patient died before receiving the treatment. “Although the deceased did not have the chance to use the medicine, we hope it can help other patients who need treatment,” she said to Bernama when met at Pusrawi Specialist Medical Centre Kuala Lumpur yesterday. Pusrawi Kuala Lumpur Specialist Hospital consultant haemato-oncologist Dr Mohd Hishamuddin Harun (second left) meets Sarimah Mingai (second right) and her husband Mahrip Onga (centre), at Pusrawi Kuala Lumpur, May 7, 2026.

items.title: [UPDATED] No rush to replace MyKad, says Home Minister
items.summary: Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the rollout of the enhanced MyKad, featuring improved security and technology, does not require existing cardholders to replace their identity cards right away. "Possessing a MyKad is mandatory under the law. However, when we introduce the new MyKad on June 1 with enhanced security features, it does not mean all existing holders must immediately replace their cards.

items.title: Two teens remanded for another week over brutal murder of college student
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Two teenagers linked to the brutal murder of a college student found with 61 stab wounds in Ketereh last week have had their remand extended by seven days. Last Friday, the body of a 19-year-old student was discovered in Kampung Padang Simah, Ketereh, with 61 stab wounds across her body. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the victim was found without identification, but was later confirmed through National Registration Department fingerprint records.

items.title: #NSTviral: Motorists praised for helping stranded woman push motorcycle in Kerinchi [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two motorists who stopped to help a woman push her motorcycle along a busy road near KL Gateway Mall have won praise from Malaysians online. A 43-second video shared on social media showed the woman struggling alone to push her motorcycle along the road towards Kerinchi, believed to be near the mall, during a light drizzle. Moments later, two men were seen getting out of their car to help push the motorcycle to safety.

items.title: Malaysia's biodiesel moment
items.summary: FOR years, Malaysia's biodiesel policy has behaved a little like a plantation road after rain - passable, promising but never entirely smooth. With B15 biodiesel production scheduled to begin from June 1 through 19 licensed plants, Malaysia is being invited to revisit a question that has never quite gone away: should palm biodiesel remain a cautious blending programme, or become a more deliberate pillar of national energy security? Malaysia's total biodiesel production capacity is about 2.4 million tonnes a year.

items.title: BNM: Targeted solutions better than monetary policy during supply shocks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Monetary policy is primarily a demand management tool. Moreover, monetary policy is a blunt policy tool that affects the entire economy. However, Abdul Rasheed said BNM remains vigilant should price pressures becoming more widespread and persistent.

items.title: Aidiladha moon sighting set for May 17 at 29 locations
items.summary: The 29 locations include Baitul Hilal Sultan Ismail Building in Pontian, Johor; the Al-Khawarizmi Astronomy Complex in Melaka; the Negri Sembilan Observatory; and Baitul Hilal sites in Bukit Melawati and Bukit Jugra, Selangor. Terengganu sites include Bukit Besar, Bukit Geliga, Pulau Perhentian, and the Kusza Observatory, while Kelantan locations include Bukit Peraksi, Bukit Kampong Tembeling, and Menara Bangunan SEDC. Pahang locations include Bukit Tanjong Batu, Gunung Brinchang, and Menara 188.

items.title: Malaysia to roll out MyNIISe to reduce congestion at Johor-Singapore checkpoint from 2027
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 8 — A new digital immigration system aimed at easing one of the world’s busiest land borders will be introduced at the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said today. He said the system’s full rollout will coincide with the launch of the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link service, which is expected to significantly improve cross-border connectivity between Johor and Singapore. “The MyNIISe system’s full rollout will coincide with the launch of the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link service, which is expected to significantly improve cross-border connectivity between Johor and Singapore,” he told reporters after reviewing the progress of the Johor Bahru-Singapore RTS Link project at the ICQS at Bukit Cagar Station here today.

items.title: Man found dead in attic following Sungai Petani house fire
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: A man was found dead following a fire at a house in Lorong Astana, Bandar Sri Astana, early today. Operations commander Mohd Zaki Senik said the team arrived at 5.07am and found the single-storey house engulfed in flames. Zaki said the victim's body was found in the attic at 5.23am.

items.title: Year Five student hurt after falling from bicycle shed roof at Melaka school, cops rule out bullying
items.summary: MELAKA, May 8 — Police have confirmed receiving a report on a Year Five pupil who was injured after falling from the roof of a bicycle shed at a primary school in Batu Berendam yesterday. Melaka Tengah police chief ACP Christopher Patit said the incident occurred at about 12.40pm during a class changeover. The boy received emergency treatment at the school before being taken to Melaka Hospital and later transferred to a private hospital for further treatment.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution: Malaysia, Singapore to share CIQ facilities when RTS Link begins 2027
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 8 — Malaysia and Singapore will implement jointly co-located customs, immigration and quarantine (CIQ) operations once the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link begins service next year, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said today. He said the arrangement will see Malaysian agencies and border security forces under the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency stationed at the Woodlands Customs Complex in Singapore. “Related agencies from Singapore will also be housed at the Bangunan Sultan Iskandar CIQ in Johor Bahru.

items.title: Targeted assistance ready for businesses hit by Middle East crisis, says BNM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Help is readily available for businesses affected by the conflict in West Asia, especially small and medium enterprises (SMEs), said Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) Governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour. Loan repayment assistance such as restructuring and rescheduling options are also available for affected borrowers. Abdul Rasheed also said businesses experiencing repayment difficulties are encouraged to engage their banks early to explore available relief options, including to pause instalments temporarily and revise repayment schedules.

items.title: Elderly man dies after Manjung home invasion
items.summary: MANJUNG: An elderly man was killed while his wife sustained serious injuries after they were attacked during a robbery at their home in Jalan Melayu, Ayer Tawar, early yesterday. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Hasbullah Abd Rahman said police received a call regarding the incident at 5.30am. Hasbullah said neighbours who heard the commotion rushed to help the victims and took them to Seri Manjung Hospital for treatment.

items.title: Home minister tells Malaysians to ‘take your time’ in switching to new MyKad
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 8 — Malaysians do not have to rush to change their MyKads when the National Registration Department (NRD) rolls out the new identification documents next month. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Malaysia has over 20 million citizens with the MyKad, adding that there is no requirement for an immediate exchange. “What we are trying to explain is that starting June 1, the new MyKad that comes with improved security features is available,” he said.

items.title: As drought grips Kedah, cloud-seeding launched to save Malaysia’s ‘rice bowl’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Malaysia is resorting to cloud seeding to bring much-needed rain to the country’s “rice bowl” north, where a drought has delayed planting of the staple crop and raised supply fears. “This year... has been affected by prolonged dry weather, low rainfall and reduced dam water levels,” the Minister of Agriculture and Food Security of Malaysia, Mohamad Sabu, told AFP. The conditions mean farmers have missed two of the three usual planting phases for so-called “wet direct seeding” of rice, a technique that requires fields to be flooded.

items.title: Penang records RM956b trade, strengthens role as key E&E export hub in Malaysia
items.summary: The Penang Yang di-Pertua Negri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib said Penang’s approved investments for 2025 totalled RM63.96 billion, citing information from the Malaysian Investment Development Authority. Out of the total approved investments, domestic investments amounted to RM38.85 billion while foreign investment amounted to about RM25.10 billion. He said Penang also recorded about RM956.37 billion in total trade for 2025, an increase of 17.9 per cent compared to the previous year.

items.title: US reputation in Malaysia hits record low as China surges, Ipsos survey finds
items.summary: By contrast, China’s positive influence rating stands at 83 per cent in Malaysia — the highest of any country surveyed and up seven percentage points from October 2025. Iran’s reputation has also risen among Malaysians, gaining four percentage points to reach 50 per cent positive perception since October last year. Indonesia mirrored Malaysia’s trend closely, with the US dropping 13 points and China commanding an 81 per cent positive rating there.

items.title: Penang must not be ‘mere observer’ in face of global economic shocks, says governor
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 8 — Penang must be proactive in strengthening its resilience by emphasising energy efficiency, strengthening food security, and ensuring disciplined and visionary fiscal management, the Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib said today. He said prolonged tensions globally, including in the Middle East, have created ripple effects on global market stability, particularly involving energy and supply chains. He said, Malaysia, as a country practising an open economic system, is not exempt from the impact of these global uncertainties.

items.title: Ketereh murder probe: Kelantan police seek to extend remand for two teen suspects
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 8 — Two teenage boys suspected of being behind the brutal killing of a 19-year-old college student found with 61 stab wounds in Ketereh were taken to court today for a remand extension. Both suspects, also aged 19, arrived at about 8.50am under police escort for further legal proceedings, Harian Metro reported. The victim, identified as Nurfisya Zulkifly, was found dead at about 12.30am on Friday along Jalan Kampung Kubang Kiat in Ketereh.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of severe weather in Klang Valley, Melaka, Perak, other states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 9 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a short-term weather warning, forecasting thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds across multiple states and federal territories until 1.00 pm. In Selangor, the affected districts include Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, and Sepang, alongside the federal territory of Putrajaya. Up north in Perak, the warning covers Manjung, Perak Tengah, Bagan Datuk, and Hilir Perak.

items.title: Guan Eng: Bank profits surged RM6.5b, time to help struggling MSMEs with relief package
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — The government should urgently channel RM5 billion in interest-free loans and a one-year loan moratorium to help struggling small businesses, Bagan MP Lim Guan Eng said today, arguing that strong banking profits justify broader support for MSMEs. “Maintaining the OPR at 2.75 per cent will help maintain the banking industry’s strong profit growth thereby justifying a RM 5 billion interest-free loans of RM 50,000 to 100,000 MSMEs and a one year interest rate moratorium for MSMEs existing loans,” he said in a statement. “The banking sector’s profits in 2025 surged by RM6.5 billion or 13.5 per cent from RM48.3 billion in 2024 to RM54.8 billion in 2025,” he said.

items.title: Tawau police uncover scam ‘training centre’ for fake stock investment scheme targeting South Koreans, nab 28 foreigners
items.summary: TAWAU, May 8 — Police busted a non-existent online stock investment syndicate targeting South Korean nationals with the arrest of 28 foreigners, including two women, in Bandar Sri Indah here yesterday. Tawau Police Chief ACP Jasmin Hussin said the syndicate is believed to have entered Sabah to carry out fraudulent activities using various instant messaging platforms. “The suspects comprised 27 Chinese nationals and one Vietnamese national aged between 21 and 51.

items.title: Selangor leads chip talent push with new Malaysia–Brazil semiconductor programme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Malaysia and Brazil have partnered to develop integrated circuit (IC) design talent through the launch of ChampionCHIP eXperience, Malaysia Edition, on Thursday. The launch, held in conjunction with Semicon Southeast Asia, underscores Malaysia’s commitment to building deep, specialised talent pipelines for the IC design sector, according to a joint statement by the Advanced Semiconductor Academy Malaysia (ASEM), the Selangor Information Technology and Digital Economy Corporation (Sidec), and the Malaysia Semiconductor IC Design Park. “The collaboration with Brazil exemplifies this vision, bringing together the expertise of ChipInventor, a leading Brazilian cloud-based IC design education platform, with Selangor’s established semiconductor ecosystem.

items.title: Sarawak Education Dept investigates electrocution case in Mukah school that killed Form Four student, critically injured another
items.summary: KUCHING, May 8 — The Sarawak State Education Department (JPN Sarawak) has initiated an internal investigation to examine compliance with safety aspects at a secondary school in Daro following an incident in which a student died from electrocution yesterday. JPN Sarawak, in a statement, said the incident was also under investigation by the authorities. JPN Sarawak also extended its condolences to the family of the deceased Form Four student, Fahrin Jumandy Abdullah.

items.title: Teen girl survives neck slashing attack in Penampang, 29-year-old suspect caught for attempted murder
items.summary: Penampang police chief Supt Sammy Newton said the 29-year-old foreign national suspect was arrested in a bush area by the roadside in Pekan Kinarut Lama, Papar, at about 5.50 pm yesterday. Sammy said preliminary investigations found that the victim had been at home with her young nephew when the suspect arrived to meet the victim’s sister-in-law over an outstanding payment for plumbing work. “The suspect allegedly grabbed the knife from the victim before slashing her neck and pushing her against the bathroom wall, causing injuries to her head and neck,” he said.

items.title: Russia calls Malaysia ‘reliable partner’ in Asia-Pacific, says Kremlin aide
items.summary: MOSCOW, May 8 — Malaysia is a reliable partner of Russia in the Asia-Pacific, with sustainable political dialogue maintained between the two countries, Russian presidential aide Yuri Ushakov said on Thursday, reported Sputnik/RIA Novosti. “Malaysia is a reliable partner of Russia in the Asia-Pacific region. Russia and Malaysia maintain regular and substantive political dialogue, including through parliaments, ministries, regions and public organisations,” Ushakov told reporters.

items.title: Pakatan to hold first national convention since 2022 in Johor on May 17, signals prep for polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 8 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) will hold its national convention in Johor on May 17, a major gathering aimed at strengthening the coalition’s machinery and outlining its strategy ahead of upcoming state polls and the 16th General Election (GE16). The convention, the first to be held since 2022, will take place at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre, the coalition announced today. As the leading coalition in the national unity government, PH said its focus remains on strengthening the national economy, ensuring political stability, and bolstering public confidence.

items.title: Boy, six, impaled by metal rod from rear seat in moving car during family trip in Baling
items.summary: Baling Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) chief Asst Fire Supt Zulkhairi Mat Tanjil said the department received an emergency call at 2.42 pm and dispatched a team to the Parit Panjang Health Clinic near here. “Part of the victim’s face was pierced by a metal rod from the rear seat. “The victim could not be removed until the metal rod was cut.

items.title: Malaysia’s oldest union MTUC hit with temporary dissolution order over missing financial documents
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 8 — The Malaysian Trades Union Congress (MTUC) has received a temporary dissolution order from the Registrar of Societies (ROS), effective yesterday, following its failure to submit several requested documents and financial records. According to the notice, dated May 7, 2026, and issued under Section 14(5) of the Societies Act 1966, the organisation was ordered to be temporarily dissolved after it was found to have failed to provide information requested by the Registrar of Societies. The notice also stated that the organisation has 30 days to submit an appeal to the Home Ministry under Section 18 of the Societies Act 1966.

items.title: Malaysia can’t sell oil to Asean because it still imports 400,000 barrels daily, says investment, trade and industry minister
items.summary: CEBU, May 8 — Malaysia does not sell oil to other Asean member states as the country is still importing about 400,000 barrels of crude oil per day, said Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani. He said although Malaysia is fortunate to be an oil-producing nation, it is still considered a net oil importer as it imports around 400,000 barrels of oil daily. Nonetheless, Johari said, when it comes to liquefied natural gas (LNG), Malaysia is still a net exporter.

items.title: Johor Regent takes part in joint land, sea and air exercise with Johor Military Force and elite special forces unit
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 8 — His Royal Highness Tunku Ismail, The Regent of Johor, took part in a joint military exercise alongside the Johor Military Force (JMF) Elite Unit and the Malaysian Army’s elite 21 Grup Gerak Khas (GGK) yesterday. GGK is the Malaysian Army’s premier special forces unit trained in counter-terrorism, jungle warfare, reconnaissance and special operations missions. The joint drill showcased tactical coordination between the JMF Elite Unit and the military special operations force through simulated combat and rapid deployment exercises.
